---
Title: "Extreme Poverty, Child Malnutrition and Under-5 Mortality Portfolio porjects"
Author: "Addisu Beyene"
Date: "2026-02-22"
Output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

##PART 1

## Step 1:  Load required packages

```{r wbc}
install.packages("tidyverse")
library(tidyverse)
install.packages("dplyr")
library(dplyr)
```
# Step 2: Read the data

```{r wbc}
wbc<-wbcc_bc
```

```{r wbc}
View(wbc)
```

Step 3: filter global health-related data only and check missing values


```{r wbc, echo=FALSE}
 
global_data<-wbc

health_data<-global_data %>% select(SH.MED.CMHW.P3, SH.STA.MALN.ZS, SH.DYN.MORT, SI.POV.DDAY)

```


## Harmonisation of the variables

```{r health_data, echo=FALSE}

health_data <- global_data %>%
  select(
    country = country,
    poverty = SI.POV.DDAY,
    malnutrition = SH.STA.MALN.ZS,
    u5_mort = SH.DYN.MORT
  )

```

# Remove rows with missing values

```{r health_data, echo=FALSE}
health_no_na <- health_data %>%
  drop_na(poverty, malnutrition, u5_mort)

```


# Filter for non-negative values

```{r health_clean, echo=FALSE}

health_clean <- health_no_na %>%
  filter(poverty >= 0, malnutrition >= 0, u5_mort >= 0)

```

 #Summary statistics
 
```{r health_clean, echo=FALSE}


summary(health_clean[, c("poverty", "malnutrition", "u5_mort")])

```
#Part 2
## Relationships between  malnutrition, poverty, and under-five mortality rate

### Correlations (to show relationships) 


```{r health_clean, echo=FALSE}
cor(health_clean[, c("poverty", "malnutrition", "u5_mort")], use = "complete.obs")
```
#PART III

# Visualise the data

# relationship between poverty and under-five mortality

# Plot 1 - Poverty vs Under-5 Mortality

```{r health_clean, echo=FALSE}

ggplot(health_clean, aes(x = poverty, y = u5_mort)) +
  geom_point(color = "steelblue", size = 3, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "red", se = TRUE) +
  labs(title = "Extreme Poverty vs Under-5 Mortality",
       x = "Poverty headcount at $2.15/day (%)",
       y = "Under-5 mortality (per 1,000 live births)") +
  theme_minimal()
ggsave("plot_poverty_vs_u5mort.png")  # Save the plot as an image

```



## Relationship between malnutrition and under-five mortality

Plot 2 - Malnutrition vs Under-5 Mortality 

```{r health_clean, echo=FALSE}
ggplot(health_clean, aes(x = malnutrition, y = u5_mort)) +
  geom_point(color = "darkgreen", size = 3, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "red", se = TRUE) +
  labs(title = "Child Malnutrition vs Under-5 Mortality",
       x = "Prevalence of underweight children under 5 (%)",
       y = "Under-5 mortality (per 1,000 live births)") +
  theme_minimal()
ggsave("plot_malnutrition_vs_u5mort.png")

```
# Plot 3 - All three: Poverty vs Malnutrition colored by U5 Mortality

```{r health clean, echo=FALSE}

ggplot(health_clean, aes(x = poverty, y = malnutrition)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = u5_mort, size = u5_mort), alpha = 0.7) +
  scale_color_gradient(low = "forestgreen", high = "firebrick") +
  scale_size(range = c(2, 9)) +
  labs(title = "Poverty vs Malnutrition (colored by U5 Mortality)",
       x = "Poverty (%)", y = "Malnutrition (%)") +
  theme_minimal()
ggsave("plot_all_three.png")

```



# Plot 4 -  with text labels for countries with very high mortality or poverty

```{r health_clean, echo=FALSE}
high_burden <- health_clean %>%
  filter(u5_mort > 60 | poverty > 50 | malnutrition > 25)

```

```{r health_clean, echo=FALSE}

ggplot(health_clean, aes(x = poverty, y = u5_mort)) +
  geom_point(color = "steelblue", size = 2.8, alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "red", se = FALSE, linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_text(data = high_burden,
            aes(label = country),
            color = "black", size = 3.2, 
            hjust = -0.1, vjust = -0.4, check_overlap = TRUE) +
  labs(
    title    = "Extreme Poverty vs Under-5 Mortality\n(with labels for high-burden countries)",
    x        = "Poverty headcount at $2.15/day (%)",
    y        = "Under-5 mortality (per 1,000 live births)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)
ggsave("plot_Povery_under5_highBur.png")
```

#PART 4. Fit the linear regression model with poverty only

```{r health_clean, echo=FALSE}
Model_under <- lm(u5_mort ~ poverty, data = health_clean)
summary(Model_under)

```

## Fit the Linear Regression Model with malnutrition & poverty

```{r health_clean, echo=FALSE}
lm_model_with_malnutrition <- lm(u5_mort ~ poverty + malnutrition , data = health_clean)
summary(lm_model_with_malnutrition)

```
It can be seen that there is statistical significant linear relationship between poverty, malnutrition and under_five mortality with a p-value <0.001.


```{r health_clean, echo=FALSE}
coef(lm_model_with_malnutrition)
confint(lm_model_with_malnutrition)
confint(lm_model_with_malnutrition, level=0.90)
confint(lm_model_with_malnutrition,parm = "poverty")

```

We can conclude that malnutrition and poverty strongly associated with under five mortality rate.


Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.
